task_summary: Add a /neuralrelay history command that displays the last 5 pipeline runs with quality scores and total token costs, reading from the .neuralrelay/handoffs/ directory.
acceptance_criteria: /neuralrelay history with completed pipeline runs displays a table with columns: chain ID, task summary, verdict, quality score, and total tokens for each of the last 5 runs
/neuralrelay history with zero completed runs prints 'No pipeline history found' and suggests running /neuralrelay start
/neuralrelay history with fewer than 5 completed runs displays all available runs without error or padding
/neuralrelay history with more than 5 completed runs displays only the 5 most recent, sorted newest-first by chain ID
/neuralrelay history gracefully handles a chain directory that contains incomplete handoffs (missing 04-review-agent.json) by showing the chain with 'incomplete' status instead of crashing
The total tokens column displays the total_tokens value from the review-final handoff, formatted with comma separators for readability
/neuralrelay history does not require neuralrelay.yaml to exist — it reads only from the handoff directory
technical_constraints: NeuralRelay is a pure markdown/JSON Claude Code plugin — the history command must be a markdown command definition in commands/history.md
Handoff chain directories use YYYYMMDD-HHmmss naming format, which means alphabetical sorting equals chronological sorting
The command must use only the tools available to Claude Code commands: Read, Glob, Bash — no external dependencies
Review-final handoffs follow schemas/review-final.schema.json — read total_tokens, quality_score, and verdict from there
Requirements handoffs follow schemas/requirements.schema.json — read task_summary from there
out_of_scope: Filtering history by date range, verdict, or quality score
Exporting history to CSV or JSON format
Showing per-agent token breakdown in the history view (that's what /neuralrelay chain is for)
Interactive selection or drill-down into individual runs
Pagination beyond the fixed limit of 5 runs
Deleting or archiving old pipeline runs

domain_glossary.chain ID: Unique identifier for a pipeline run, formatted as YYYYMMDD-HHmmss timestamp
domain_glossary.handoff chain: The complete set of handoff JSON files produced by a single pipeline run, stored in .neuralrelay/handoffs/{chain-id}/
domain_glossary.quality score: 1-10 rating from the review agent's review-final handoff, assessing overall implementation quality
domain_glossary.total tokens: Sum of tokens used across all agents in a pipeline run, recorded in the review-final handoff
